title: Bugfix: Plugin feedback loop prevention (race condition, re-entrancy guard, debounce)
description: When the plugin is active, OpenCode spams sessions at ~3/sec, blocking the app and crashing the machine. Six-layer defense-in-depth fix: (1) Move injectedSessions.add() BEFORE await client.session.prompt() to close race window in injectContext — rollback on failure. (2) Add isRefreshing re-entrancy guard to refreshAndDiff so it cannot call itself via cascading events. (3) Replace direct refreshAndDiff calls in tool.execute.after and session.idle with scheduleRefresh() using 500ms trailing debounce. (4) Filter chat.message to only inject into orchestrator sessions — mark non-orchestrator sessions in injectedSessions immediately. (5) All hooks early-return if !activated. (6) Prevent concurrent activation with activating flag.
close_reason: Implemented 5-layer feedback loop prevention: race condition fix (injectedSessions.add before await), re-entrancy guard on refreshAndDiff, debounced scheduleRefresh replacing direct calls, orchestrator-only chat.message filter, !activated early-return in all hooks

title: Bugfix: Plugin lazy activation (dormant until orchestrator activity or /dashboard command)
description: The plugin factory function runs startupSequence() immediately at load time, spawning the dashboard server, registering, starting heartbeats, and fetching beads every time OpenCode starts — even when the user is not using the orchestrator pipeline. Fix: add activated/activating flags, remove startupSequence() from factory body, auto-detect orchestrator via chat.message hook (input.agent === orchestrator), add command.execute.before hook for manual /dashboard activation, add !activated early-return to all other hooks.
close_reason: Implemented lazy activation: removed startupSequence from factory body, added activated/activating flags, auto-detect via chat.message when agent=orchestrator, manual /dashboard command via command.execute.before

title: Pipeline badge shows LIVE when session is disconnected
description: When an opencode session disconnects, the project shows 'Disconnected' (red badge) but the pipeline still shows 'LIVE' (green badge). The pipeline status should transition from 'active' to 'idle' when the session disconnects. Fix in 3 locations: server/state.ts deregisterPlugin(), server/state.ts loadFromDisk(), and src/hooks/useBoardState.ts PROJECT_DISCONNECTED action.
close_reason: Closed

title: Hide live elapsed time counter for Backlog and Done columns
description: The ElapsedTime component in BeadCard.tsx (line 111) renders unconditionally for all cards. A live ticking counter is not meaningful for Backlog (items waiting to be picked up) or Done (work already finished). Wrap the ElapsedTime render in a conditional: bead.stage !== 'backlog' && bead.stage !== 'done'.
close_reason: Closed

title: Collapsible pipeline board height — default to single-card compact mode
description:
When a pipeline has many beads (especially in Backlog), the entire board row stretches tall, wasting vertical space. Add a compact/expanded toggle per pipeline.

## Behavior
- **Default: Compact mode.** Board height constrained to ~1 card tall (~120-130px). Content clipped with overflow-hidden (no scroll in compact mode). Only the top card in each column is visible.
- **Expanded mode:** Full natural height, same as current behavior (ScrollArea per column).
- **Toggle:** A small expand/collapse icon (ChevronDown/ChevronUp) on the PipelineHeader row, next to the pipeline title, status badge, and bead count.
- **Per-pipeline state:** Each pipeline independently tracks compact/expanded, so you can expand one while others stay compact.
- Optionally persist to localStorage per pipeline ID.

## Files to change
- src/components/PipelineHeader.tsx — Add expand/collapse toggle button. Accept and invoke an onToggle callback + isExpanded prop.
- src/components/Board.tsx — Accept isExpanded prop. When compact, wrap the column flex container in a div with max-h ~120-130px and overflow-hidden. When expanded, no height constraint (current behavior).
- src/components/ProjectSection.tsx — Manage per-pipeline expanded state (useState or localStorage-backed). Pass isExpanded + onToggle to PipelineHeader and Board.

## Notes
- Column headers should remain visible in compact mode (they're part of the column component, inside the constrained area — this is fine since column headers are at the top).
- The min-h-[120px] on Column.tsx line 91 should be removed or reduced in compact mode so columns don't force extra height.
close_reason: Closed

title: Improve plugin deregistration reliability on shutdown
description:
The plugin's deregisterFromServer() in dashboard-bridge.ts:478 is fire-and-forget. If the process exits before the async fetch completes, the DELETE never reaches the server. 

Fix: Use keepalive:true on the fetch request so it survives process shutdown. Also consider adding process exit hooks to ensure deregistration happens before the process terminates.
close_reason: Added keepalive:true to fetch and improved exit hooks - deregistration now survives process shutdown

title: Add project:connected SSE event handling to frontend
description:
BUG: The server broadcasts 'project:connected' when a plugin registers (routes.ts:146), but the frontend never handles it. This means when a user closes an opencode session and reopens one, the badge stays 'Disconnected' forever.

Fix requires changes in 3 files:
1. shared/types.ts - Add 'project:connected' to SSEEventType union and add SSEProjectConnectedPayload interface
2. src/hooks/useEventSource.ts - Add 'project:connected' to the eventTypes array (line ~126)
3. src/hooks/useBoardState.ts - Add PROJECT_CONNECTED action type, reducer case (set connected:true, update lastHeartbeat/pluginId), and SSE_TO_ACTION mapping
close_reason: Fixed project:connected SSE event handling - badge now updates on reconnect

title: Polish: Error detection and error column

All 216 tests pass, dark mode compatible.

title: Reduce heartbeat timeout for faster disconnect detection
description:
When the plugin process exits abruptly, the DELETE deregister call (fire-and-forget in dashboard-bridge.ts:478) often doesn't complete. The server falls back to heartbeat timeout detection: 90s timeout checked every 30s = up to ~120s worst-case delay before 'Disconnected' badge appears.

Fix: Reduce PLUGIN_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS from 90_000 to 45_000 in server/routes.ts:449. This cuts worst-case detection from ~120s to ~75s while still allowing for 1 missed heartbeat (heartbeats sent every 30s).
close_reason: Reduced heartbeat timeout from 90s to 45s - faster disconnect detection (~75s vs ~120s)

title: Hide completed/empty projects from dashboard with toggle
description:
When all beads in a project are done (or there are 0 beads), hide the project from the dashboard to reduce clutter — especially important when monitoring multiple projects.

## Behavior
- A project is 'completed' when it has zero non-done beads across all pipelines (includes 0-bead projects).
- Completed projects are hidden by default.
- A toggle at the top of the dashboard shows: 'N completed projects hidden [Show]'.
- When toggled on: 'Showing N completed projects [Hide]'.
- Persist toggle state to localStorage (key: dashboard:showCompleted).
- If a completed project receives a new bead, it automatically reappears without toggling.
- If ALL projects are completed, show the toggle (not EmptyState).

## Files to change
- src/App.tsx — filter projects into active/completed, add toggle state (localStorage-backed), render toggle UI at top of dashboard near header area.
- Optionally a helper isProjectCompleted(project) in src/lib/ or inline.

## No backend changes needed.
close_reason: Closed
